title: Molten Herbal Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Tunisian dish featuring tender lamb in a rich and flavorful herbal sauce, served molten and nostalgic on a wooden board.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: boneless and cut into 2 inch pieces

instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the lamb, olive oil, onion, garlic, thyme, rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Transfer the lamb mixture to a large Dutch oven or heavy pot with a tight fitting lid.
Add the kombu cold extract to the pot and stir to combine.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the lamb for 2.5 hours, or until it is tender and falls apart easily.
In a small bowl, mix together the cornstarch and 2 tablespoons of water until smooth.
Stir the cornstarch mixture into the pot and continue to cook for an additional 30 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the lamb and sauce molten on a wooden board.
